mission.title: kserve: [kubeflow 1.3] unable to route requests to the kfserving pod due to auth policy
mission.description: [kubeflow 1.3] unable to route requests to the kfserving pod due to auth policy. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify kserve troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kserve deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve -n kserve --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kserve configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kserve configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get configmap -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kserve
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
deploy and inferenceserving example

I am installing in a preexisting knative deployment and perhaps I'm missing some steps.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [kubeflow 1.3] unable to route requests to the kfserving…
steps.description:
I also had to add `/v1/models/*` path to allow Transformer->Predictor requests.

Without it the Transformer gets a 403 forbidden
```BASH
$ curl -v -H "Cookie: authservice_session=$SESSION" -H "Host: ${SERVICE_HOSTNAME}" -d $INPUT_PATH
```yaml
{"level":"error","ts":"2021-04-24T19:10:16.241Z","logger":"activator","caller":"net/revision_backends.go:322","msg":"Failed to probe clusterIP 172.30.171.229:80","knative.dev/controller":"activator","knative.dev/pod":"activator-886cd96fb-4gqq5","knative.dev/key":"raffa/flowers-sample-predictor-default-00002","error":"unexpected body: want \"queue\", got \"RBAC: access denied\"","stacktrace":"knative.dev/serving/pkg/activator/net.(*revisionWatcher).checkDests\n\t/opt/app-root/src/go/src/knative.dev/serving/pkg/activator/net/revision_backends.go:322\nknative.dev/serving/pkg/activator/net.(*revis
```

steps.title: Upgrade kserve to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kserve:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kserve kserve/kserve --namespace kserve
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve
helm list -n kserve
```

steps.title: Confirm [kubeflow 1.3] unable to route requests to the… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve -n kserve --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kserve --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I also had to add `/v1/models/*` path to allow Transformer->Predictor requests.

Without it the Transformer gets a 403 forbidden
```BASH
$ curl -v -H "Cookie: authservice_session=$SESSION" -H "Host: ${SERVICE_HOSTNAME}" -d $INPUT_PATH http://${INGRESS_HOST}:${INGRESS_PORT}/v1/models/$MODEL_NAME:predict
...

spec:
  rules:
    - when:
        - key: 'request.headers[kubeflow-userid]'
          values:
            - raffa
    - when:
        - key: source.namespace
          values:
            - raffa

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
 name: test-service
spec:
 selector:
   matchLabels:
     serving.kubeflow.org/inferenceservice: test-service  
 rules:
   -  {}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kserve installed or the issue environment reproducible.
